data.title: Bank7 signals 4.40%-4.45% core NIM outlook as it targets moderate single-digit loan growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bank7 Corp. (BSVN) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "we are happy with our results today" and "we are really proud of our ability to manage our NIM and to properly mix our balance sheet, and we're not concerned about rates going out or rates going up, we're positioned either way" (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Thomas Travis).
  • On growth expectations, management kept its annual lens intact: "our goals for the year remain intact. We're still thinking moderate single digit" while noting pace versus late 2025: "coming off of the third and fourth quarter we had last year where we had really robust growth... we're not at that pace" (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Jason Estes).
  • On energy exposure and demand sensitivity, management emphasized it is not a swing factor for the story: "it's at a 10-year low... it was 8 -- a little over 8% of the portfolio" and "I wouldn't expect the energy piece to be causing a lot of dynamic change" (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis).
  • "we are modeling in that same range, 4.40% to 4.45% from a core NIM perspective, and on the loan fee side of things, kind of reverting back to the normal of 28 to 35 basis points" (Executive VP & CFO Kelly Harris).

Outlook

  • "we are modeling in that same range, 4.40% to 4.45% from a core NIM perspective" and loan fees "reverting back to the normal of 28 to 35 basis points" (Executive VP & CFO Harris).
  • For Q2 operating cadence, management provided internal ranges: "For Q2 on the expense side, we're projecting internally in the range of $9 million to $9.2 -- and on the fee side, low end of $750,000, upwards of $850,000" (Executive VP & CFO Harris).
  • On the oil-and-gas-related gross-up, management framed it as temporary and not earnings-driving: "continued the expense offsetting the income. So not really material to the bottom line, but temporarily grossing up both sides of the P&L" (Executive VP & CFO Harris).

Financial Results

  • The prepared remarks did not state GAAP revenue, EPS, or net income figures in the transcript; management instead pointed listeners to "the major metrics in the deck" (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis).
  • Margin drivers called out in Q&A included deposit-side progress: "We did make some really good progress on the liability side, cost of funds, and that was related to our talented bankers continuing to bring in some quality core deposits" (Executive VP & CFO Harris).
  • Management quantified quarter items affecting recurring margin discussion: "from a core NIM perspective, I think the nonaccrual interest net up was $1.1 million" and "on a fee perspective, it's closer to 1.7" (Executive VP & CFO Harris).

Q&A

  • Adam Kroll, Piper Sandler: asked whether loan growth expectations changed after payoffs pressured period-end balances; Executive VP Estes replied, "our goals for the year remain intact. We're still thinking moderate single digit" and added, "We expect pretty sizable payoffs" in Q2 that would be offset by bookings.
  • Adam Kroll, Piper Sandler: asked about NIM ex-fees with stable rates; Executive VP Harris answered, "we are modeling... 4.40% to 4.45%" core NIM and loan fees "28 to 35 basis points."
  • Adam Kroll, Piper Sandler: asked about capital build, M&A, and tolerance for higher capital; President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis said, "we ended the quarter at 15.96% on risk base" and "we've always been active in the M&A space" for "the right strategic opportunities."
  • Will Jones, KBW: asked if deposit competition could push deposit costs higher later in the year; President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis said, "I don't see a massive fluctuation or any meaningful fluctuation in deposit costs" assuming no rate increase.
  • Will Jones, KBW: pressed on provisioning/reserves sustainability; Executive VP Estes said, "our credit book is as clean as it's ever been" and outlined sensitivity: "if we grow the book... yes, we'll have to provision a little bit more" while slower growth could reduce the need.
  • Will Jones, KBW: asked again on buybacks; President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis said, "share buybacks really don't add franchise value" and framed any future use as conditional: "driven by a good share repurchase price and no other alternatives."
  • Jordan Ghent, Stephens: asked for downgrade detail; Executive VP Estes said, "we had a large builder developer relationship... and that was the one Tom referenced that we think will pay off this week."
  • Jordan Ghent, Stephens: revisited M&A approach including MOE; President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis said, "I think the answer is both" and "we've not deviated from our thinking on that."
  • Nathan Race, Piper Sandler: asked about oil-and-gas fee/expense impacts; Executive VP Harris reiterated gross-up mechanics, and President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis added, "we have accomplished our goal" and expects it "either gone altogether or diminished quite a bit over the next few months."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral-to-slightly skeptical, with repeated probing on sustainability (deposit costs, normalized margin, provision outlook, and capital returns), including "what is the messaging on the provision and reserve levels going forward?" (Will Jones, KBW).
  • Management’s tone was confident in positioning and execution, emphasizing steadiness: "we're positioned either way" on rates (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis) and "it's steady as she goes for Bank7" in closing.
  • Versus last quarter’s emphasis on rate cuts and margin downside scenarios, management’s language shifted toward stability and less rate-path sensitivity, with "we don't expect anything materially different" on margin (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Rate narrative shifted: in Q4 2025 management discussed "a couple of rate cuts" and potential NIM pressure with more cuts, while in Q1 2026 management noted markets "were expecting rate cuts" but now "maybe the rates will go the other way" and asserted, "we're positioned either way" (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis).
  • Loan growth messaging moved from 2025’s "really robust growth" backdrop to a moderated pace while keeping targets: "still thinking moderate single digit" and "we're not at that pace" (Executive VP Estes).
  • Capital return posture stayed consistent: Q1 2026 reiterated buybacks as non-core—"don't add franchise value"—while highlighting ongoing M&A intent and a high capital position (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis).

Risks and Concerns

  • Macro uncertainty was repeatedly flagged as the swing factor for rates and credit: "the increase in commodity prices associated with the Middle Eastern conflict" (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis) and "we really don't know what the economy is going to do for the rest of the year" (Executive VP Estes).
  • Credit risk discussion centered on monitoring and specific resolution prospects: "we have quoted a payoff for this Friday" tied to a material remaining NPA, and if it closes, "the net effect would be NPAs of somewhere in that $4 million to $5 million range" (President, CEO & Vice Chairman Travis).

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized balance-sheet positioning for a shifting rate outlook, maintained expectations for “moderate single digit” loan growth despite expected payoffs, and guided to a “4.40% to 4.45%” core NIM range with loan fees normalizing to “28 to 35 basis points.” Capital remained high at “15.96% on risk base,” with continued M&A interest and a consistent stance that buybacks “don't add franchise value,” while oil-and-gas-related income/expense was described as a temporary gross-up expected to be “gone altogether or diminished quite a bit over the next few months.”

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Canadian Copper secures C$96M project financing from OR Royalties, Ocean Partners
data.description:
Canadian Copper (CNDIF) up 15.6% in Tuesday's trading after saying it secured as much as C$96M in committed capital from OR Royalties (OR) and Ocean Partners U.K. to develop its 100%-owned Murray Brook project and Caribou process plant—together called the Bathurst complex—in Canada's New Brunswick province.

OR Royalties' (OR) share of the funding will total C$38M in exchange for a 20% life-of-mine payable silver and gold stream from Bathurst, Ocean Partners—an existing 17% shareholder in Canadian Copper (CNDIF) - will provide up to C$48M in project debt in exchange for 100% of Bathurst's offtake rights, and the companies will provide up to C$10.5M in equity subscriptions.

OR Royalties' (OR) will provide Canadian Copper (CNDIF) with C$28M in cash proceeds for the precious metals stream.

Canadian Copper's (CNDIF) preliminary economic assessment from July 2025 outlined a 13.2-year mine plan for the Murray Brook deposit that proposed an open pit with four development phases at an average production rate of 3,300 metric tons/day of mineralized material at a strip ratio of 5.0:1 for 13.2 years.

data.title: TransDigm shares rise after upbeat preliminary results signal strong quarter
data.description:
Shares of TransDigm Group (TDG) rose as much as 5.6% Tuesday after the aerospace supplier pre-announced stronger-than-expected fiscal second-quarter results, prompting a positive reaction from analysts.

In a research note, Matt Akers of BNP Paribas said the early figures point to “solid” underlying performance, reinforcing expectations for continued growth across the company’s key segments.

Preliminary numbers

TransDigm (TDG) said it expects revenue of about $2.54 billion to $2.545 billion for the quarter, ahead of both BNP Paribas estimates of $2.445 billion and broader Wall Street expectations near $2.417 billion.

The company also forecast earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of roughly $1.33 billion to $1.335 billion, exceeding both the firm’s estimate and consensus projections.

Akers noted that the stronger profitability, combined with share repurchase activity during the quarter, could translate into higher earnings per share than previously modeled.

Capital activity in focus

The preliminary results were disclosed alongside a proposed $1.25 billion debt transaction tied to an acquisition and roughly $800 million in share buybacks completed during the quarter.

The analyst said those buybacks were not previously incorporated into forecasts, suggesting potential upside to earnings estimates once full results are released.

Growth signals across segments

While the company didn't provide detailed commentary on business trends, Akers said the performance likely reflects steady demand across its end markets.

BNP Paribas had been projecting about 10% overall growth for the quarter, including similar gains in aftermarket sales, stronger growth in original equipment and mid-single-digit expansion in defense.

The update may also bode well for other aerospace suppliers, particularly those with exposure to the aftermarket, which has been supported by high aircraft utilization and maintenance demand.

Outlook

BNP Paribas maintained its Outperform rating on TransDigm (TDG), with a price target of $1,700, implying significant upside from recent levels.

Akers said the firm will revisit its forecasts when the company provides full earnings details in the coming weeks, but added that the early read suggests no signs of a slowdown in the business.

data.title: Google's DeepMind launches more advanced Gemini robotics
data.description:
Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) DeepMind AI research lab introduced its recently upgraded Gemini robotics model and is making the system available to developers via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio.

Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 is the latest iteration of Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) reasoning-first model that can understand their environments with “unprecedented precision" over predecessors ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash. 

“By enhancing spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding, we are bringing a new level of autonomy to the next generation of physical agents,” Google said in a statement.

Through these advancements, Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 specializes in precise object detection, spatial and physical reasoning capabilities, motion detection, and success detection that allows a robot to choose between retrying a failed attempt or progressing to the next stage of a task.

Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 can also achieve highly accurate instrument readings by using agentic vision. This combines visual reasoning with code execution by first zooming into an image, and then using pointing and code execution to estimate proportions and intervals to get a precise reading.

To build an even more robust model, Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) invites the robotics community to provide specific mode failures of the Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 “to ensure Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 provides maximum value.”

data.title: Airbus shareholders back board changes as chair succession set for October
data.description:
Shareholders of Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) approved all resolutions at the company’s 2026 annual general meeting, endorsing board appointments, a dividend payout and a planned leadership transition at the top of the board.

The meeting, held Tuesday in Amsterdam, comes after what the company described as a pivotal 2025 across its commercial aviation, helicopter and defense businesses.

Board changes

Investors approved the renewal of board mandates for Mark Dunkerley, Stephan Gemkow and Antony Wood, each for three-year terms. The meeting also confirmed several changes to the non-executive lineup.

Henriette Hallberg Thygesen, chief executive of Terma A/S, joined the board as a non-executive member, replacing Feiyu Xu, whose term expired at the close of the meeting. Xu had contributed to discussions on digitalization and artificial intelligence, according to the company.

Oliver Zipse, chief executive of BMW, was appointed as a non-executive member for a one-year term to complete the mandate of Victor Chu, who is stepping down after eight years.

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) said its staggered appointment system, under which directors are elected in blocks each year, is designed to avoid large-scale turnover at a single meeting and preserve continuity.

Dividend approval

Shareholders also approved a dividend of €3.20 per share for the 2025 financial year, in line with the company’s proposal.

Chair transition set

The company confirmed that René Obermann will step down as chair of the board on October 1, and will not seek re-election as a director when his mandate expires in 2027.

He will be succeeded by lead independent director Amparo Moraleda, who has been designated to take over the role on the same date.

The transition follows nearly a decade of leadership by Obermann, during which Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) navigated the COVID-19 pandemic, supply chain disruptions and heightened geopolitical tensions.

Outlook and context

The governance changes come as Airbus seeks to maintain momentum following a strong prior year and position itself for continued demand in civil aviation while managing ongoing challenges in defense and space operations.

The company said its current structure, including an experienced board and management team, is intended to support stability as it faces a shifting global environment shaped by industrial, geopolitical and technological pressures.

data.title: S&P 500 jumps 2% and these 20 stocks are fueling the rally
data.description:
U.S. equities advanced sharply on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) climbing 2% to trade near 6,954, bringing the benchmark within 0.6% of its all-time high of 7,002.28 set on January 28.

The rally marks a significant recovery in market sentiment, as the index has now fully retraced losses incurred during the recent U.S.-Iran conflict.

Strength across major sectors has supported the move, reflecting renewed investor confidence and easing geopolitical concerns. With momentum building, market participants are closely watching whether the S&P 500 can decisively break above its previous record in the near term. 

Outlined below are the top 20 performing stocks within the S&P 500 on Tuesday:

  1. Robinhood Markets (HOOD), +9.9% 
  2. Delta Air Lines (DAL), +7.6%
  3. Coinbase Global (COIN), +6.9% 
  4. Super Micro Computer (SMCI), +6.6%
  5. Southwest Airlines (LUV), +6.3% 
  6. Axon Enterprise (AXON), +5.7%

  10. Bio-Techne (TECH), +4.7%

data.title: Southern Copper CEO Rocha dies unexpectedly
data.description:
Southern Copper (SCCO) said late Tuesday that President, CEO, and board member Oscar Gonzalez Rocha died unexpectedly on April 7 and plans to appoint a successor or interim CEO in the near future.

Rocha was Southern Copper's (SCCO) President and CEO since 2004 and was a director of the company since 1999, after serving as President and CEO of Americas Mining Corp. and CEO and a director of Asarco LLC.

Rocha also headed Grupo Mexico's (GMBXF) mining division, consolidating the conglomerate's position as one of the most important mining groups in the region.

Southern Copper (SCCO), one of the world's largest copper producers, is 88.9%-owned by Grupo Mexico (GMBXF).

data.title: Equity longs ease as Hormuz blockade reshapes positioning, oil bets build
data.description:
Long positioning across U.S. equity indices has softened as traders reassess risk amid the ongoing US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, while bullish bets on oil continue to build.

According to data from Capital.com, the share of traders long the S&P 500 (SP500) has slipped to 61% from 64% earlier in the week, while positioning in the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) dropped to 60% from 67%. The Russell 2000 stands at 52%, close to flipping net short, and the Nasdaq 100 (COMP:IND) has moved into a slight bearish bias.

Positioning is more mixed globally. Long exposure in the DAX has eased, while traders increased bullish bets on the ASX 200 and Nikkei 225.

In commodities, Capital.com data shows long positions in gold have been trimmed on profit-taking, while WTI crude oil longs have risen on expectations of continued supply disruption.

“Tuesday morning’s data shows a broad reduction in long bias across US equity indices as the price gains built on optimism that an eventual deal will be made took some buyers by surprise who opted to take profit, with a similar story occurring in gold,” said Monte Safieddine of Capital.com, adding that oil longs remain resilient while USD/JPY traders continue to favor the short side near the 160 level.
